Adversarial L1 reviewer only sees tool evidence (first 400 chars of result).

commit.message:
Merge pull request #11 from dchu3/fix/telegram-confirmation-deadlock

fix: resolve confirmation deadlock in Telegram bot

grammY's bot.start() processes updates sequentially — the message
handler was awaiting runAgent(), which awaited the confirmFn Promise
(waiting for an inline keyboard tap). But the button tap is a callback
query update that grammY couldn't process until the message handler
returned, creating a deadlock that always timed out.

Fix: Run agent processing as a fire-and-forget async task so the
message handler returns immediately, allowing grammY to process
callback query updates concurrently.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: security hardening — path traversal, log leaks, race condition, error exposure

Tier 1 security fixes from TECH_DEBT_AUDIT_2026Q1:

- Path traversal (3 files): Replace inline canonicalize checks with
  PathSecurityUtils.validateExtractionPath() for defense-in-depth
- Sensitive log leaks (5 files): Wrap debugPrint/logDebug of UDP messages,
  device fingerprints, AI request bodies, clipboard content, SQL queries
  with kDebugMode guards
- Race condition: Rewrite _executeWithLock to use for(;;) with re-check
  after each await, preventing multiple waiters from acquiring the lock
- Error info leaks (5 files): Guard e.toString() in user-facing SnackBars

commit.message:
network: Remove outbound masternode reconnect loop

Masternodes are servers. When one comes back online it runs Phase 1
on startup and connects outbound to its peers. There is no reason for
every other node to continuously hammer a potentially-offline masternode
with outbound connection attempts every 120 seconds.

Phase 1 (startup) still connects to all registered masternodes.
After that, masternode mesh topology is maintained purely through each
node's own outbound connections on startup/recovery — no polling needed.

The Phase 3 loop now only fills regular peer slots, as before.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
network: Exponential backoff cooldown for persistently unreachable peers

Previously, the reconnection cooldown was a flat 10 minutes regardless
of how many failure cycles a peer had gone through. This caused nodes
like 34.170.12.145 to be retried endlessly: fail 3 times, 10-min
cooldown, retry, fail again, 10-min cooldown, repeat forever.

Changes:
- adaptive_reconnection.rs: Scale cooldown exponentially per repeated
  failure cycle: base 600s * 2^(extra_cycles), capped at 24 hours.
  Example: 3 failures=10min, 5=40min, 7=2.7h, 9+=24h max.
- client.rs Phase3-MN: Add AI pre-check before mark_connecting+spawn
  for masternodes (same pattern already used for regular peers). Avoids
  spawning tasks that immediately exit due to cooldown, eliminating
  spurious log noise and unnecessary task churn.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: fuzzy filter now actually filters the tree

Previously the / filter accepted input but the tree didn't narrow:
- flattenNode returned early when a node was not expanded, so children
  were never searched when a filter was active
- Only direct name matches were shown; ancestors of matching nodes
  were dropped (no context breadcrumbs)

Changes:
- When filter is active, always recurse into children regardless of
  expanded state so the full subtree is searched
- Show a command row if it directly matches OR has any matching
  descendant (preserves ancestor breadcrumb context)
- Add nodeOrDescendantMatchesFilter() helper
- Export RowCount() on TreeModel for testing
- Add 8 unit tests covering: match, ancestors, hidden non-match,

commit.message:
Fix ARM64 register clobbering in buffer copy and SHA1

Separate byte_at() calls from store8() calls to avoid the Novus V0.1.3
ARM64 compiler bug where the second argument to a function call
clobbers the first argument's computed value in the register.

Affected functions: my_buf_copy, my_write_cstr, sha1_update,
sha1_process_block loop, sha1_concat, and network read loops.

commit.message:
Break search-value ties using static evaluation instead of random selection (#39)

* Initial plan

* Initial analysis of failing test nextShouldBlockOpponentOpenFour

Co-authored-by: zhixiangli <8456250+zhixiangli@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix searchBestPoint to use evaluatePointValue as tiebreaker instead of random selection

When the alpha-beta search returns the same value for multiple candidate moves
(e.g., all moves lead to a loss against an unstoppable threat), the agent now
breaks ties using the static evaluation (evaluatePointValue) instead of randomly
selecting among tied candidates. This ensures the AI prefers strategically
important moves (blocking opponent threats, completing patterns) even when the
search cannot differentiate between them.

commit.message:
Merge pull request #10 from dchu3/fix/stale-callback-query

fix: handle stale Telegram callback queries gracefully

When the 60s confirmation timeout fires before the user taps a button,
the callback query handler would call answerCallbackQuery on an expired
query, causing a GrammyError that crashed the bot.

Fix:
- Add .catch() to answerCallbackQuery and editMessageText in the
  callback handler since these Telegram API calls can fail on stale
  queries
- Add bot.catch() global error handler as a safety net so uncaught
  middleware errors log instead of crashing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
